variant.handshake_hint: Use tls-token for maximum active-probe resistance (M8).

generation_result.reasoning: This configuration uses TLS 1.3 with a Chrome 120 fingerprint and ALPN settings to mimic a real browser. The handshake is embedded in the TLS session ticket, which is a standard field, to avoid detection by active probes (M8). Padding is set to mimic Chrome browsing traffic to pass flow-based ML classifiers (M4) and KL divergence of packet lengths (M6). No artificial timing is applied to avoid worsening inter-arrival time divergence (M7). A fallback reverse-proxy to www.google.com ensures active probes receive valid HTTPS responses. The chosen SNI and target are consistent to avoid Suricata alerts (M2).
known_weaknesses: Using a fixed SNI (www.google.com) might be less common in enterprise settings and could be monitored.
Port 9443 is non-standard for HTTPS and might attract attention, though it is commonly used for secure services.
The session ticket field carrying 48 bytes of handshake data might be slightly longer than average, though session tickets can vary.
